item.title: Adnoc subsidiary completes acquisition of leading oilfield services player
item.description: UAE-based drilling giant will expand its rig fleet to 170 units, the largest in the Middle East, following the acquisition

item.title: Samsung scoops $330 million prize for Malaysia's first LNG FSRU project
item.description: MISC will own and operate key liquefied natural gas import facility for Petronas Gas

item.title: 28 Tcf: Critical second wave of Southeast Asian deepwater gas projects
item.description: However, fragile economics weigh on more than $20 billion-worth of investments targeted by 2030

item.title: Chinese yards lined up for upgrade of iconic FPSO
item.description: Operator's choice will determine whether 40-year-old Petrojarl 1 can be reliably overhauled within tight 10-month window

item.title: BP steps up India deepwater push, eyes key offshore development
item.description: BP is a key partner at KG-D6, India’s largest gas-producing deepwater field, operated by private sector giant Reliance Industries

item.title: Oil and gas do not face an 'existential threat’, says Guyana's president
item.description: President Mohamed Irfaan Ali says his country has adopted an integrated, dual-path approach to deal with energy transition

item.title: Singapore's Keppel keen to complete unfinished offshore drilling rigs
item.description: Market conditions are supporting Keppel's new focus on unfinished rigs

item.title: All is well as Equinor secures contractors for $1.8 billion
item.description: 18 services companies required to support the wells Norwegian operator will drill

item.title: UK contractor awarded subsea deal in Australia
item.description: JDR Cable Systems to supply umbilicals for project in the Otway basin

item.title: Adnoc chief says UAE’s decision to exit Opec not ‘directed against anyone’, will accelerate investments
item.description: The UAE recently left Opec and the wider Opec+ grouping, amid unprecedented disruption to Middle East crude production and shipping through the Strait of Hormuz

item.title: Namcor hunts partner to drill 2-billion-barrel prospects in Orange basin block
item.description: Petroleum Exploration Licence 79 lies in very shallow water

item.title: Supermajor considering North Sea exit
item.description: BP mulling sale of UK assets

item.title: Supermajor considering $2.7 billion North Sea exit
item.description: BP mulling sale of UK assets

item.title: Adnoc outlines plan for $55 billion project awards, days after Opec exit
item.description: Adnoc is expanding its oil production capacity to 5 million barrels per day and is also spending heavily to scale up Abu Dhabi’s gas production

item.title: Angkor ups the ante in Cambodia
item.description: Toronto-listed junior preparing for drilling on key onshore asset

item.title: Oil prices drift slightly lower on hopes of US-Iran talks
item.description: President Trump confirmed the US is still in negotiations with Iran but gave no indication of firm progress

item.title: Production begins at eighth FPSO in Brazil’s largest offshore field
item.description: P-79 has capacity for 180,000 barrels per day of crude

item.title: US LNG player backed by oil heavyweights offers timeline for first gas into export terminal
item.description: NextDecade sees second half of 2026 as target for introducing feedgas at Rio Grande LNG

item.title: ExxonMobil CEO: ‘Market hasn't seen the full impact’ of Middle East war
item.description: US supermajor still working with QatarEnergy to assess liquefied natural gas facility damage

item.title: Occidental CEO Hollub to retire following 40-year career; US player names successor
item.description: Chief operating officer Richard Jackson to take top post 1 June

item.title: Junior player places $155 million bet on Nova Scotia offshore blocks
item.description: Canadian province's premier welcomes Inceptio Oil & Gas' bid for two blocks

item.title: Chevron beats earnings estimates
item.description: Elevated oil prices helped boost upstream results

item.title: ExxonMobil profits fall as derivatives obscure upstream strength
item.description: US supermajor saw higher production in the quarter as Guyana set new record

item.title: Equinor exec who led oil giant's UK wind power push departs
item.description: Executive had led the oil and gas giant’s UK renewable energy push, including to build one of the world’s biggest offshore wind farms

item.title: Australian junior keeps shareholders waiting on Namibia farm-out news
item.description: Pancontinental Energy says 'invited candidates' have accessed data room for Orange basin licence

item.title: Woodside and Bolivia top this week's leading oil and gas appointments
Modec division secures yard capacity in Malaysia
item.description: Sofec and MMHE agree to long-term collaboration

item.title: Modec division secures agreement with yard in Malaysia
item.description: Sofec and MMHE agree to long-term collaboration

item.title: Thailand's top operator ramps up gas output to full capacity
item.description: PTTEP raises production to 2.72 billion cubic feet per day in response to Middle East crisis

item.title: Japanese player set to re-enter Petronas gas project
item.description: Petronas and Eneos sign 'definitive agreements' formalising latter's re-entry into LNG venture
